\chapter*{Education}

\begin{itemize}
\item Ph.D in Economics, University of North Carolina - Chapel Hill, 2025 (Expected)
\item M.A. in Economics, Duke University, 2019
\item B.S. in Economics and Mathematics, Belmont University, 2016 (\emph{Summa Cum Laude})
\end{itemize}

\chapter*{Research experience}

\begin{itemize}
\item Summer-Fall 2022: Research Assistant 

\begin{itemize}
\item Personal RA for NERA Affiliated Consultant
\item Duties included: Formatting, cleaning, and combining electricity auction data using Microsoft Excel. \\
\item Supervisor: Chantale LaCasse
\end{itemize}
\item Summer 2021: User Behavior and Economics Research Intern

\begin{itemize}
\item CableLabs
\item Duties included: Developing a time-use model of consumer demand for internet usage 
\item Supervisor: Jacob Malone
\end{itemize}
\item Summer 2020 - Present: Research Assistant

\begin{itemize}
\item University of North Carolina - Chapel Hill
\item Duties included: Working on research topics around forecasting intermittent, demand with integer-valued outcomes, airline upgrade auction design, and market definitions.
\item Supervisor: Jon Williams
\end{itemize}
\item Summer 2020 - Fall 2020: Research Assistant

\begin{itemize}
\item University of North Carolina - Chapel Hill
\item Duties included: Cleaned and managed data from Pharmaprojects and IMS in Stata
\item Supervisor: Luca Maini and Andres Hincapie
\end{itemize}
\item Fall 2017 - Spring 2019: Research Assistant

\begin{itemize}
\item Duke University
\item Duties included: Identified acquisitions in raw data, managed data in SAS, and ran regressions in Stata.
\item Resulting paper: \href{https://academic.oup.com/qje/article-abstract/135/1/221/5607794?redirectedFrom=fulltext}{How Acquisitions Affect Firm Behavior and Performance: Evidence from the Dialysis Industry}
\item Supervisor: James Roberts
\end{itemize}
\end{itemize}
